data.title: Oil opens new year with strong gains on optimism over economic rebound in China
data.description:
Crude oil futures rose for a fifth straight session Friday to close the week near a three-month high, helped by optimism over the potential for further stimulus in China, the world's largest crude importer.

Crude kicked off the new year with strong gains this week, sparked by a pledge from China's President Xi Jinping for more proactive economic policies to boost growth in 2025.

China also announced new measures intended to promote growth with a surprise move to raise wages for government workers and a sharp increase in funding from ultra-long treasury bonds.

After worries about Chinese demand were a major factor in bearish oil demand assumptions last year, "China just is unceasing at this point in terms of their announcements about trying to stoke economic activity, and the market is taking note of that," Again Capital's John Kilduff said.

Trading sentiment is cautious around geopolitical risks on the horizon, particularly ahead of President-elect Trump's upcoming inauguration, but geopolitical uncertainty and a potential Chinese economic resurgence are aligning positively in early 2025, contributing to the upward move in oil prices, Pepperstone's Ahmad Assiri wrote.

"Although the average market outlook for 2025 suggests crude oil prices will hover around the mid-$70s/bbl, geopolitical risks will remain a recurring factor capable of driving oil prices toward new equilibrium levels," according to Assiri, adding that greater optimism around industrial activity in China "may challenge the fundamental assumptions underpinning market forecasts."

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for February delivery finished the week +4.7% to $73.96/bbl, and front-month March Brent crude (CO1:COM) closed +3.7% to $76.51/bbl this week, the highest settlement value for both benchmarks since mid-October; on Friday, WTI and Brent gained 1.1% and 0.7%, respectively.

U.S. natural gas futures fell as near-term temperatures are not seen as cold as previously forecast, while last week's inventory draw of 116 Bcf was smaller than expected, although enough to put storage below year-ago levels and reduce the surplus over the five-year average; front-month February Nymex natgas (NG1:COM) plunged 8.4% on Friday to $3.354/MMBtu, turning the week negative at -0.8%.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

Energy stocks, as represented by the Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (NYSEARCA:XLE), surged this week, +3.4%.

Top 20 gainers in energy and natural resources in the past 5 days: FTC Solar (FTCI) +44.3%, Gevo (GEVO) +39.7%, Zeo Energy (ZEO) +31.9%, Scully Royalty (SRL) +31.8%, Montauk Renewables (MNTK) +24.7%, Kosmos Energy (KOS) +23%, Energy Vault (NRGV) +22.8%, North European Oil Royalty Trust (NRT) +21.8%, Nabors Industries (NBR) +20.8%, GeoPark (GPRK) +20.2%, Polar Power (POLA) +19%, StealthGas (GASS) +18.7%, Ameresco (AMRC) +17.2%, SolarBank (SUUN) +17%, NextDecade (NEXT) +16.9%, Lightbridge (LTBR) +16.8%, Sunnova Energy (NOVA) +16.1%, Knot Offshore Partners (KNOP) +14.8%, Calumet (CLMT) +14.4%, Imperial Petroleum (IMPP) +14.4%, Array Technologies (ARRY) +13.8%.

Top 5 decliners in energy and natural resources in the past 5 days: Stardust Power (SDST) -22%, American Battery Technology (ABAT) -21.3%, Eco Wave Power (WAVE) -21.2%, Nexa Resources (NEXA) -18%, Martin Midstream (MMLP) -15.2%.

Source: Barchart.com

data.title: Novo Nordisk seeks to block compounded Victoza
data.description:
Novo Nordisk (NOVO) is petitioning the FDA to exclude its diabetes drug liraglutide, also known as Victoza, from a list of drugs that are allowed to be compounded.

In a Citizen Petition posted on Regulations.gov, Novo Nordisk (NVO) noted that the Outsourcing Facilities Association, or OFA, has nominated liraglutide be added to their list of bulk drug substances that may be used in compounding. The drugmaker is asking the agency to exclude liraglutide from the list.

"Liraglutide does not meet the standard and criteria set forth by Congress and the FDA for inclusion on the 503B Bulks List. Liraglutide is not a bulk drug substance for which there is a 'clinical need' to compound," the company said in its petition.

Novo Nordisk (NVO) markets liraglutide under the brand names Victoza, Saxenda and Xultophy. The drug is currently on the FDA's drug shortage list.

Compounding pharmacies are allowed to make versions of branded drugs if they are considered to be in short supply by the FDA. Compounded drugs are not FDA approved.

In its petition, Novo Nordisk (NVO) noted that Teva (NYSE:TEVA) already produces an authorized generic version of liraglutide. Last week, the FDA approved a second generic version of liraglutide, which will be marketed by Hikma Pharmaceuticals.

Novo Nordisk's (NVO) petition is its latest move in a battle to keep cheaper, copycat versions of its lucrative diabetes and weight-loss drugs off the market.

In October, Novo Nordisk (NVO) asked the FDA to prevent compounding pharmacies from producing versions of its blockbuster GLP-1 drug semaglutide, which is marketed for weight loss as Wegovy and for diabetes as Ozempic. Some formulations of the drug remain on the FDA's shortage list.

Earlier this week, rival Eli Lilly (LLY) filed a motion with a federal court to join in on a case filed by the OFA over the FDA's removal of Lilly's (LLY) popular GLP-1 drug tirzepatide from its shortage list, arguing it couldn't rely on the FDA to protect its interests. Lilly (LLY) markets the drug for weight loss as Zepbound and for diabetes as Mounjaro.

data.title: Stellantis subsidiary FCA US reports Q4, annual sales
data.description:
• Stellantis (NYSE:STLA) subsidiary FCA US reported U.S. Q4 2024 retail sales increase of 1% Y/Y.
  • Jeep brand's Q4 2024 U.S. retail sales increased 6% Y/Y.
  • FIAT brand's 2024 total calendar year sales increased 153% Y/Y, led by sales of the all-electric Fiat 500e.
  • FCA US reported sales of 320,743 vehicles in the Q4, down and 1.3M vehicles for 2024.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Biden issues permit for Perpetua's Idaho antimony and gold mine - Reuters
data.description:
Perpetua Resources (NASDAQ:PPTA) +9.2% post-market Friday after Reuters reports the Biden administration issued the final mining permit for the company's Stibnite antimony and gold mine in Idaho.

The administration published the final record of decision - essentially the mine's permit - through the U.S. Forest Service for Perpetua's (PPTA) project after an eight-year review process, according to the report.

The move would be an attempt to challenge China's near-total control of the market for antimony, a metal used widely across the defense and energy sectors.

data.title: Range Capital Acquisition announces full exercise of over-allotment option in its IPO
data.description:
• Range Capital Acquisition (NASDAQ:RANG) announced that its IPO underwriters fully exercised the over-allotment option, issuing an additional 1.5M units at $10 each.
  • After the option was exercised, a total of 11.5M units were issued in the IPO, raising $115M.
  • Each unit consists of one ordinary share and one right, entitling the holder thereof to receive one-tenth of one ordinary share upon the completion of an initial business combination. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Top S&P, Nasdaq winners as Wall Street breaks losing streak with 2025 action underway
data.description:
Friday’s rally on Wall Street allowed the S&P 500 (SP500) and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) to break their longest string of declines in months.

The equity benchmark (SP500)(SPY)(VOO) piled on 1.3%, and the Nasdaq (COMP:IND) leapt 1.8%, after the past five consecutive sessions concluded in the red. The losing streak was the lengthiest since April, when each average slumped 4%, and logged its first negative month since October 2023.

What’s been “holding back animal spirits” lately is uncertainty about the incoming Trump administration’s policy stances, but market prices overall appear “bullish to our eyes,” Alex King, investing group leader of Cestrian Capital Research, told Seeking Alpha on Friday.

Friday’s top 10 percentage gainers on the S&P 500 (SP500):

  • Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI): +10.9%
  • Vistra (NYSE:VST): +8.5%
  • Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA): +8.2%
  • Palantir (NASDAQ:PLTR): +6.3%
  • NRG Energy (NYSE:NRG): +6.2%
  • Jabil (NYSE:JBL): +4.8%
  • Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA): +4.45%
  • GE Vernova (NYSE:GEV): +4.45%
  • Monolithic Power (NASDAQ:MPWR): +4.3%
  • Constellation Energy (NASDAQ:CEG): +4%

And Friday’s top 10 percentage advancers on the Nasdaq (COMP:IND):

  • MicroStrategy (NASDAQ:MSTR): +13.2%
  • Arm Holdings (NASDAQ:ARM): +10%
  • Tesla (TSLA): +8.2%
  • Palantir (PLTR): +6.3%
  • Nvidia (NVDA): +4.45%
  • Marvell Technology (NASDAQ:MRVL): +4.1%
  • Constellation Energy (CEG): +4%
  • Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD): +3.9%
  • MercadoLibre (NASDAQ:MELI): +3.9%
  • Applied Materials (NASDAQ:AMAT): +3.8%

All 11 sectors on the S&P 500 (SP500) rose Friday, paced by the Consumer Discretionary group +2.4%, and Information Technology +1.6%.

data.title: NETCLASS TECHNOLOGY announces full exercise of over-allotment option
data.description:
• NETCLASS TECHNOLOGY (NASDAQ:NTCL) announced that underwriters of its IPO fully exercised their over-allotment option, purchasing an additional 270,000 Class A shares at $5 each, raising an extra $1.35M in gross proceeds.
  • After the over-allotment option was fully exercised, the company sold 2,070,000 Class A shares, raising total gross proceeds of $10.35M.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Mazda North American Operations reports full-year sales up over 16% to 424,382 vehicles
data.description:
• Mazda North American Operations (MNAO) (OTCPK:MZDAY) on Friday reported total full-year sales of 424,382 vehicles, an increase of 16.8% compared to 2023.
  • The company reported total December sales of 40,201 vehicles, a 1.7% Y/Y compared to December 2023.
  • Mazda Canada reported December sales of 4,566 vehicles, an increase of 2.4 percent compared to December last year. Full-year 2024 sales increased 23.2 percent, with 72,226 vehicles sold.
  • Mazda Motor de Mexico reported December sales of 8,677 vehicles, an increase of 19 percent compared to last year. Full-year 2024 sales increased 31 percent, with 99,797 vehicles sold.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: U.S. grain futures pressured as export sales fall below forecasts
data.description:
U.S. grain futures fell sharply on Friday after export sales of soybeans and wheat reported by the Department of Agriculture came in below expectations.

In its latest weekly export sales report, the USDA said soybean export sales for the week ended December 26 totaled 484.7K metric tons in the 2024-25 marketing year, down 51% from the previous week and a marketing year low, and wheat sales totaled 140.6K tons in 2024-25, also a marketing year low and down 77% from the previous week.

Analysts surveyed by The Wall Street Journal had forecast soybeans to fall by 500K-1M tons, and wheat to land in the range of 200K-525K tons; corn sales came in at the low end of forecasts, totaling 777K tons for 2024-25.

Wheat for March delivery (W_1:COM) finished -3.2% on Friday to $5.28 1/2 per bushel on the Chicago Board of Trade, March soybeans (S_1:COM) settled -2% to $9.92 per bushel, and March corn (C_1:COM) closed -1.9% to $4.51 1/4 per bushel.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:WEAT), (NYSEARCA:SOYB), (NYSEARCA:CORN), (DBA), (MOO)

Soybeans and soy products have been under pressure on concerns over renewable fuel programs around the world, not just in the U.S., where President-elect Trump is expected to advocate more for fossil fuels.

"Delayed implementation of Indonesia's B40 program is a headwind for vegoils, with palm oil down nearly 2% on the day," John Stewart & Associates wrote, adding that "without 45Z guidelines in the U.S. we may see [soybeans] continue to face headwinds here in the short run."

data.title: Boeing appoints Dana Deasy as chief information officer
data.description:
• Boeing (NYSE:BA) on Friday announced the appointment of Dana Deasy as the company's new chief information digital officer and senior vice president, Information Technology & Data Analytics.
  • Most recently, Deasy served as chief information officer at the U.S. Department of Defense and before that, at JPMorganChase, BP and General Motors.
  • Deasy replaces Susan Doniz, who left the company late last year.
  • BA +0.04% after hours to $169.98.
  • Source: Press Release
